findings.claim: Distractor-D rationale presents a passage phrase in single quotes that is not verbatim.
findings.evidence: Rationale D: "tempting because 'rising into an aircraft's path' is named." The passage reads "the flocking species that rise in numbers into an aircraft's path" — the quoted fragment 'rising into an aircraft's path' does not appear verbatim (the passage has 'rise in numbers into', not 'rising into').
findings.why: The integrated read requires quoted phrases to appear VERBATIM. This is a loose paraphrase inside quote marks. It is substantively accurate (the concept of birds rising into the aircraft path IS in the passage) and does not affect the key, the passage, or any option text, so it does not overturn the CONSISTENT verdict — a real-but-minor observation only.
